title: Rankett Agency Partnership — white-label tiers, agency-arbitrage pitch
html:
Rankett Agency Partnership — white-label tiers, agency-arbitrage pitch

Source: 2025-12-30 Google Doc. The agency-channel offer doc for Rankett — companion to the same day Direct Offer sales script. Defines the white-label partnership program with three pricing tiers.

TL;DR

  • The pitch: Rankett is a fully white labeled AI visibility engine for marketing/SEO agencies. Plug into existing workflows in a week. Agency keeps the relationship and margin; Rankett runs the engine.
  • The arbitrage frame: Rankett's direct rate is $3,500/mo per client. Agency wholesale starts at $3K for 1-2 clients, drops to ~$600/client at scale. Agency charges client $3-6K. Margin = $2-4K per client per month.
  • Three tiers:
    • 🥇 Starter (1-2 clients): $3K/mo flat. One client covers cost; second is mostly margin. De-risked from day 1.
    • 🥈 Growth (3-6 clients): $7,500 base + per-client. At 6 clients = ~$900/client effective wholesale. "This is where healthy, scalable partnerships live."
    • 🥇 Scale (7-10+): $8,800 base + $800/client beyond 10. At 20 clients = ~$550/client. "$49K-$89K margin/month at scale."
  • What the agency gets: AI visibility engine running behind scenes, white-label dashboard on agency domain, standardized client onboarding flow, sales templates / positioning guides / training videos, Rankett team support.
  • What the client sees: "Agency Name's Proprietary AI Visibility Engine" — never sees Rankett. Includes monthly visibility audits, competitor benchmarking, ROI metrics.
  • Onboarding portal roadmap: Q2 2026 = dedicated agency partner portal (one-click new client, branded intake forms auto-sent from agency domain, real-time deliverable tracking, billing across accounts).
  • Pilot CTA: Pick 2 clients you trust, run them on Tier 1 ($3K total). If they upgrade, you've already made profit. If they don't, cancel partnership. "No risk."
  • The Enablement Kit (4 docs): (1) Close-the-Agency script for Yahir, (2) White-Label Sell Sheet for agency-to-client, (3) Upsell Talk Track for monthly review, (4) Monthly Update Email Template.
  • Key phrase to know: AI visibility, share of voice, brand authority, sentiment analysis, competitor share — the agency's vocabulary for selling this.

Pattern observed

This is the Rankett channel-strategy doc — pairs with the Direct Offer to form the dual-track GTM (direct-to-business + agency-channel). The agency channel is the scaling lever — direct sales cap at how many calls Yahir can run himself; agencies multiply per-client unit economics.

The Tier 1 → Tier 2 → Tier 3 unit-cost-curve is the clearest proof Rankett is being designed as a wholesale infrastructure play, not a SaaS app — language explicitly pivots from "sign up" to "apply to partner" in the April Rankett.com build doc.

The "Document 3: Upsell Talk Track" is interesting — it scripts the agency's pitch to existing clients, leaning on fear of being invisible to AI as the wedge for a $1-3K retainer increase. Same fear-frame Yahir uses in the Direct Offer.

The Agency Partnership doc + Direct Offer doc + the April build doc form the Rankett sales-collateral trilogy — together they document the entire commercial surface of the business as of late 2025/early 2026.

Key quotes

  • "Stop Leaving $2-4k/Month Per Client on the Table."
  • "Your job is to sell. This kit is built so you can. We removed all the noise: no email sequences, no marketing assumptions, no fluff."
  • "You're not a SaaS tool — you're a private infrastructure network for agencies that qualify."

Open questions

  • Has any agency actually signed at Tier 2 or Tier 3? (The April Rankett.com doc lists 3 free-work clients but no paying agency partners yet — suggests pre-traction.)
  • The "Q2 2026 onboarding portal" — built? On track?
  • The $3,500/mo direct-rate — does it match what was actually closed, or is it aspirational anchor pricing?
